nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Peshale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Cornish: locative name from any of more than two dozen places called Penhale in Cornwall, or from places of similar name in Wales, such as Pennal (Merioneths), or from Penheale in Egloskerry (Cornwall). The Cornish places named Penhale derive from Middle Cornish * pen hal ‘moor’s head, marsh’s head’ ( pen ‘head, top, end; promontory’, hal ‘moor, marsh’); Pennal (Merioneths) has the same meaning. Penheale derives from Middle Cornish pen + hel ‘hall’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Peshale was born in 1138, in England, his father, Robert de Peshale, was 20 and his mother, Ormunda de Lumley, was 20. He had at least 5 sons with Alice FitzAlan.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Peshale est né en 1138, à Angleterre, son père, Robert de Peshale, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Ormunda de Lumley, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils avec Alice FitzAlan.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de Peshale 1138 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Peshale 20 und seine Mutter Ormunda de Lumley 20. Er hatte mit Alice FitzAlan mindestens 5 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de Peshale è nato il nel 1138 nel England, il suo padre, Robert de Peshale, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Ormunda de Lumley, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli con Alice FitzAlan.
